Ridgeline Trail Guide

The Ridgeline Trail is a 12 kilometer loop through the Blackwood Hills, gaining about 600 meters of elevation. Most hikers complete it in 4 to 6 hours.

Gear

Bring at least 2 liters of water per person, sturdy hiking boots with ankle support, and a headlamp in case you're still on the trail at dusk. Trekking poles are recommended for the steep descent near the old quarry.

Weather

The trail is exposed along the ridge for about 3 kilometers, so check the forecast before heading out. Lightning storms roll in quickly during summer afternoons, and the ridge section has no shelter.

Wildlife

Hikers regularly spot deer near the lower meadow. Black bear sightings are rare but have been reported near the creek crossing at mile 4. Store food in a sealed container and make noise on the trail to avoid surprising wildlife.

Permits

No permit is required for day hikes. Overnight camping along the ridge requires a free permit from the ranger station, available up to 24 hours in advance.
